item.title: My Favorite Podcast Apps - Android + Windows
item.description: Jump to: ⭐ Pocket Casts – AntennaPod – Podcast Addict – Grover – Switching Where I listen Mostly on Android – my phone or occasionally tablet, followed by my laptop (Windows 10/ Linux). What’s important to me Trim silent audio, syncing playback/ listening time and history for at least Android and Windows 10. ⭐ Pocket Casts This is the app I’m using now. There are tons of customization/ listening/ player settings.

item.title: Install Doom Emacs on Windows 10
item.description: I’ve been using Emacs on and off since… last October? (2019) During that time, it was mainly the vanilla GNU Emacs, and I tried Spacemacs, but I could never install Doom Emacs for some reason. Now that I know more, (but still next to nothing), I can, and I’ll try and walk you through it, on Windows 10, without WSL or anything too fancy. I’m using Powershell in Windows Terminal.

item.title: Personal
item.description: Email earnestma@outlook.at – GPG Key Resources Discord Nextcloud Meetings on the official Jitsi Meet instance – cyndepftr3

item.title: Hosting your Hugo Site with Github Pages
item.description: A lot of things happened this July. First, I wanted to move my Lettered Plans website away from Wix. While it looked nice and everything, it did load quite slowly, and the drag-and-drop editing was kind of too tedious for my needs. Second, why not also have a personal site for random posts like these!? At that time, I had recently stumbled across some video on static website generators, then after found Hugo.

item.title: Workspacer + Tiling Window Managers on Windows 10
item.description: Tiling Window Managers If you’re familiar with Linux operating systems, you may have used one or seen a tiling window manager at some point. If you haven’t, according to Wikipedia, a tiling window manager is a window manager with an organization of the screen into mutually non-overlapping frames, as opposed to the more popular approach of coordinate-based stacking of overlapping objects (windows) that tries to fully emulate the desktop metaphor.

item.title: About
item.description: Jump to: Me – Site Info – What I Use Site Info Built with Hugo using the Hugo theme “terminal”, delivered to you using Github Pages. Previously, I used the Anatole theme. Copyright Unless otherwise stated, This work by earnest ma is licensed under CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 What I use Laptop Asus VivoBook F512D External Storage - WD 2TB Hard drives File Storage/ Sharing Nextcloud, Onedrive, Google Drive
